video:video.video:title: Israeli military strikes in southern Lebanon in intense fighting as US-Iran talks postponed
video:video.video:description: Israel’s military said Friday its forces struck targets throughout southern Lebanon overnight as Hezbollah reported intense fighting in the area, threatening the nascent agreement between Iran and the United States to end their war.

video:video.video:title: Bears and elephants at Berlin Zoo cool off in heatwave
video:video.video:description: Animals at Berlin Zoo were given frozen treats to help them cool off as the German capital sizzled in scorching temperatures on Friday. (AP video Fanny Brodersen)

video:video.video:title: Towns in southern Lebanon around Nabatiyeh hit by new Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military launched multiple strikes inside southern Lebanon on Friday morning as Hezbollah reported intense fighting in the area.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine buries 13 unknown soldiers at the National Military Memorial Cemetery
video:video.video:description: The fallen, whose identities have not yet been established, were buried with full military honors, part of an effort to ensure dignified commemoration and to establish a lasting national practice of honoring unknown soldiers.

video:video.video:title: Obamas open presidential museum in Chicago
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama formally opened his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday with a call to defend democracy as three former presidents joined him on stage in an extraordinary event featuring politicians, A-list celebrities, athletes and other internationally known figures.

video:video.video:title: Meloni slams Trump's claim she 'begged' for a photo with him
video:video.video:description: Italy’s government on Friday slammed U.S. President Donald Trump’s claim that Premier Giorgia Meloni had “begged” for a one-on-one meeting with him during the recent G7 summit, a pushback that suggested the longtime U.S. ally had had enough of Trump’s boasting.

video:video.video:title: Who is Andy Burnham, the lawmaker seeking to replace Keir Starmer?
video:video.video:description: The 56-year-old politician presents himself as an amiable northern everyman who prefers T-shirts to a suit and tie and spends spare time playing soccer or spinning 1990s tunes during DJ battles.

video:video.video:title: Worshipers in Tehran skeptical US will honor initial deal with Iran to end war
video:video.video:description: Worshipers in Tehran on Friday expressed skepticism about Iran's initial deal with the U.S. to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Dhole puppies receive their first medical check at Budapest Zoo
video:video.video:description: The dhole puppies at Budapest Zoo took center stage on Friday as they were given their first proper medical check-up since they were born back in April.

video:video.video:title: How to avoid World Cup ticket scams online
video:video.video:description: Looking for a World Cup ticket? The buzz around the soccer tournament now under way across North America might inspire a last-minute search online, but fans should be wary of cyber scammers.

video:video.video:title: Cambodian Supreme Court upholds incitement conviction of opposition politician in charged case
video:video.video:description: Cambodia’s Supreme Court on Friday upheld the incitement conviction of a prominent opposition politician while suspending the remainder of his sentence, keeping him out of prison but unable to practice politics for years.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Is Milly Alcock's 'Supergirl' LGBTQ+?
video:video.video:description: In a tribute to Pride Month, Milly Alcock ponders whether Kara Zor-El - aka Supergirl - identifies as LGBTQ+, as she attends a fan event for the new movie in London.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 19
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday June 19th: U.S.-Iran talks postponed; Israeli military strikes in southern Lebanon in intense fighting; U.S. strike on alleged drug boat; Tropical storm remnants drench Gulf states.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of women march for justice for sexual violence victims in Nagaland
video:video.video:description: The small mountainous state of Nagaland, which has consistently ranked as one of the safest places for women in India, has been shaken by a series of sexual assaults in recent months.

video:video.video:title: Aerials show emergency workers attending to train collision injured in UK
video:video.video:description: Emergency services rushed to the scene of a collision Friday between two trains north of London and south of the town of Bedfordshire on lines connecting St.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 19
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, June 19th: President Trump unveiled new Air Force One; ceasefire announced between Israel and Hezbollah; new guidance issued for ships in Strait of Hormuz; Barack and Michelle Obama surprise Presidential Center visitors

video:video.video:title: Meet Osito, the rescue dog who steals hearts at the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Joining Merlin the duck in the pantheon of heart-stealers is Osito, an 8-year-old rescue poodle mix who’s become an unlikely sensation after arriving at Mexico’s opening match, dressed in a Mexico jersey, sunglasses and a cap.

video:video.video:title: Trump acknowledges support from allies at new Air Force One unveiling
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump thanked China and Middle Eastern allies for their support during the conflict with Iran on Friday as he unveiled the new Air Force One.

video:video.video:title: Historic significance of Juneteenth celebrated by Galveston, Texas residents
video:video.video:description: Residents in Galveston, Texas turned out to celebrate the deep roots of Juneteenth in that city. June 19, 1865 marks the day when the last enslaved African Americans in the Confederacy learned of their freedom there.

video:video.video:title: AI, robotics and quantum computing take centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
video:video.video:description: AI, robotics and quantum computing are taking center stage at VivaTech 2026, as one of Europe’s largest technology events returns to Paris.

video:video.video:title: Barack and Michelle Obama greeted the first visitors of the new presidential center on Juneteenth
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama and former first lady Michelle Obama surprised the first 100 visitors to walk through the doors of Obama’s new presidential center, personally greeting them.

video:video.video:title: Labour Party’s Andy Burnham wins UK special election, setting up likely push to oust PM Keir Starmer
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham is a political insider turned outsider who aims to be Britain’s next prime minister. Burnham is expected to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer after winning a seat in Parliament in a special election he hailed as a “turning point” for U.K. politics.

video:video.video:title: Dragon boat festival brings annual fearsome competition to Hong Kong
video:video.video:description: Dozens of boats raced in Aberdeen, in the south of Hong Kong Island, during a public holiday - many dressed up, including one team which wore costumes depicting seafood, and legendary Chinese hero Ne Zha.

video:video.video:title: A conflict over cattle in Brazil's Amazon highlights tensions for Indigenous peoples
video:video.video:description: In northern Brazil, a debate over cattle and conservation is unfolding on Bananal Island. The situation highlights the challenge of balancing conservation, Indigenous rights, and agribusiness.

video:video.video:title: Cancellation of Iran-US signing ceremony in Switzerland leaves next step in talks unclear
video:video.video:description: Talks planned for Friday in Switzerland between Iran and the United States, which Vice President JD Vance had been scheduled to attend, were postponed as the fighting intensified in southern Lebanon, with Israel’s military claiming Friday its forces struck Hezbollah targets throughout the region.

video:video.video:title: More than 3000 Malawian nationals deported in South Africa following widespread anti-immigration protests
video:video.video:description: Malawi is the latest among at least three African countries to facilitate the repatriation of some of their citizens from South Africa as frustrations over illegal migration in the country worsen and some foreign national express fear of violent attacks.

video:video.video:title: Hotel with thousands of tourists burns in Dominican Republic
video:video.video:description: Emergency authorities in the Dominican Republic had to evacuate approximately 1,700 guests on Friday after a fire broke out at a hotel in a tourist town east of the Caribbean nation, it was officially reported.

video:video.video:title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, says ships are 'pouring out' Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump showed off the new Air Force One, a formerly Qatari-owned jumbo jet that has been converted into the official U.S. presidential aircraft.

video:video.video:title: Trump unveils the new Air Force One, a converted Qatari jet
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Friday showed off the new Air Force One, a formerly Qatari-owned jumbo jet that has been converted into the official U.S. presidential aircraft.

video:video.video:title: Scenes of joy in Mexico City as fans celebrate World Cup win over South Korea
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans filled streets across the capital Thursday night after Mexico edged South Korea 1-0 in a World Cup group-stage match, moving the host nation closer to a place in the knockout round.

video:video.video:title: In Accra, a Juneteenth gathering turns slave trade memory into a new reparations push
video:video.video:description: African and Caribbean leaders in Ghana on Friday urged former slave-trading nations to issue apologies and reparations over the trafficking of enslaved Africans after a landmark U.N. resolution in March declaring it “the gravest crime against humanity.”

video:video.video:title: Italian foreign minister cancels US trip after Trump's 'serious and offensive' remarks toward Meloni
video:video.video:description: Giorgia Meloni for her part posted a video calling Trump’s claims “completely fabricated” and expressing astonishment that he would invent such things about an ally.

video:video.video:title: Passengers recount UK train crash which killed one, injured dozens
video:video.video:description: Passengers described scenes of chaos and confusion on Friday as emergency crews responded to a fatal train collision north of London.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese in Tyre and Israelis on border reflect on ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Residents in Lebanon and Israelis along the border on Saturday voiced uncertainty over the interim peace agreement between the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Colman Domingo imagines life in Ralph Lauren
video:video.video:description: At the Ralph Lauren show in Milan, actor Coleman Domingo praised Ralph Lauren's versatile looks. (June 20)

video:video.video:title: Indonesia's first giant panda cub Rio makes public debut at zoo in Cisarua
video:video.video:description: Panda enthusiasts flocked to Cisarua Zoo in Indonesia on Saturday to catch a glimpse of giant panda cub, Rio, the first of its kind in the country.

video:video.video:title: Founder of global gaming company Ubisoft killed in plane crash in France
video:video.video:description: Claude Guillemot, one of the founders of the global gaming company Ubisoft, the maker of Assassin’s Creed, and his flight instructor have been killed in a plane crash in western France, authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Two children, one aged four, killed in Israeli attack on central Gaza
video:video.video:description: A four-year-old and a 14-year-old have died after an Israeli strike hit an apartment in the central Gaza Strip at around 2 a.m local time Saturday.

video:video.video:title: US-Iran talks set to go ahead but Tehran closes strait over Lebanon fighting
video:video.video:description: Iran dealt two quick blows to the interim agreement with the United States on Saturday, angered by Israel's continued attacks in Lebanon, saying it had closed the Strait of Hormuz and announcing that while its negotiators were going to Switzerland for talks, not much is likely to happen there.

video:video.video:title: Thousands march in gay pride march in Switzerland's largest city
video:video.video:description: Revelers, some in drag, others in colorful outfits or wearing rainbow-colored face paint, marched under sunny skies in Zurich to express their support for LGBTQ rights.

video:video.video:title: India's viral Cockroach Party increases pressure on education minister
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of students and young supporters of India’s viral Cockroach Janta Party banged steel plates with spoons in a protest Saturday to demand the resignation of the education minister over allegations of examination irregularities and repeated paper leaks, adding to the pressure on Prime Minister

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 20
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Saturday, June 20th: Iran says it’s closed the Strait of Hormuz again; UK train collision killed one driver and left nine in critical condition; Bolivia’s president declares a state of emergency; Indonesia’s first giant panda cub, Rio, makes public debut at zoo.

video:video.video:title: Bolivia president declares state of emergency as blockades choke supplies
video:video.video:description: Bolivian military personnel on Saturday began clearing access routes to the capital, La Paz, in the neighboring city of El Alto following a presidential order issued under the country's state of emergency decree.

video:video.video:title: Bridge jump daredevils seek relief from heat in Paris
video:video.video:description: Crowds of young people improvised a game of cat and mouse with local police, as they flocked to the Saint-Martin Canal in Paris on Friday for a diving contest off of a footbridge that authorities have desperately tried to close off.

video:video.video:title: In Taylor Swift’s beach town, every clue becomes a wedding rumor
video:video.video:description: Rumors that Taylor Swift was planning to marry at her Rhode Island home this weekend have drawn fans, photographers and curious visitors to the seaside village of Watch Hill after a large tent appeared near her estate.

video:video.video:title: Newly released video captures the aftermath of a fatal teen stabbing at a Texas track meet
video:video.video:description: Newly released video from an officer's body camera and a surveillance camera at a Texas running track captures the moments after a teenage athlete fatally stabbed another teen from a rival team in the stadium bleachers during a high school meet last year.

video:video.video:title: JD Vance departs for Switzerland as White House looks to get talks with Iran back on track
video:video.video:description: U.S. and Iranian negotiators headed to a Swiss venue Saturday for talks on adding key details to their interim agreement to halt the war, hours after Tehran said it closed the Strait of Hormuz because of Israel’s attacks in Lebanon and warned that little might be achieved if the fighting doesn’t sto
